finding: Perm routing changed from `conn.is_admin || check_permission_union(&user,&groups,p)` to `principal.is_admin() || principal.has_permission(p)`. SyncConnPrincipal.active_group_permissions filters is_active groups; Principal::has_permission default = check_permissions_array(direct) OR per active-group check_permissions_array — identical to check_permission_union.

SyncEntity/SyncAction/SyncEvent/SyncConnectedData/SyncSseEvent (all #[derive(JsonSchema)], all in OpenAPI surface) did NOT move and are byte-unchanged; framework never names them. types.ts (ui+desktop) BYTE-IDENTICAL; openapi.json canonically-equal.

Considered generic SyncEvent<E> — rejected: schemars renames instantiation SyncEvent_for_SyncEntity → types.ts drift. SyncEntityKind trait abstracts ONLY the registry-synthesized session event (Event, not a schema type), keeping wire types concrete.

deliver_session_to_users<E> replaces inline SyncSseEvent::Sync(SyncEvent{Session,Update,uid}) with E::session_signal(uid); ziee's impl builds the identical expression. Skip-origin + per-user-id + single-lock semantics preserved. Framework test asserts targeting+origin-skip; prune-lagging test preserved.
deliver's origin_conn skip (Some(cid)==origin → return) preserved verbatim; framework test origin_connection_is_skipped_but_other_tabs_are_not passes.

GLOBAL(512)/PER_USER(12)/CHANNEL(1024) caps + 429 AppError codes (SYNC_GLOBAL_LIMIT/SYNC_USER_LIMIT) unchanged; Full/Closed try_send→prune via shared remove_conn on both deliver + session paths. Framework tests: per_user_cap, global_cap(429), lagging prune (both paths).

deliver now takes a pre-built axum Event (was SyncEvent). publish serializes once app-side then forwards; registry clones per-conn as before → one serialization, N clones, identical. Only caller of deliver/deliver_session_to_users is ziee's publish/publish_session_to_users (grep-confirmed).

lazy_static REGISTRY:SyncRegistry<SyncConnPrincipal> + registry() stay in ziee (a generic registry can't own a monomorphized global). Framework provides SyncRegistry::new(). Matches B4 (Repos app-side) / B3 (app-installed resolver). registry() return type pinned to concrete P.

SyncRegistry<P:Principal>; new/register/… require P:Principal+Send+'static so lazy_static Sync holds; Default bound aligned with new()'s bound (fixed a first-pass E0599). deliver_session_to_users<E:SyncEntityKind> is a method-level generic. sdk cargo check --workspace + framework tests green.

framework sync deps: ziee_core::AppError, ziee_identity::{Principal,PermissionCheck,PermissionList,check_permissions_array}, axum, tokio(sync), uuid, aide(OperationIo on SyncOrigin). Added tokio+uuid to Cargo.toml. lib.rs pub mod sync + crate-root re-export. cargo check -p ziee-framework exit 0.

Move made 3 imports dead under -D unused-imports: event.rs PermissionList (removed w/ Audience), permissions/mod.rs PermissionList re-export (T-7 drop), permissions/types.rs bin-tree PermissionList (T-8 #[allow]). All reconciled during drift loop; both binaries + sdk workspace clean.

14 registry routing tests + 2 audience constructor tests moved to framework, re-expressed over TestPrincipal (collapses direct+group into Principal) + TestEntity + dummy Events (routing/pruning don't inspect payload). Group-derived routing kept via active_group_permissions override. Added 1 evaluator-sanity test. ziee keeps the wire-format tests (event.rs) + check_permission_union union tests (checker.rs).

mod.rs re-exports unchanged: pub use event::{Audience,SyncAction,SyncEntity,publish,publish_session_to_users}; pub use extractor::SyncOrigin. All ~40 emit sites' `use crate::modules::sync::{...}` (+ one sync::event::{...} in file_rag/ingest.rs) resolve unchanged. Zero emit-site edits.

B2 deferred AppEvent/EventBus 'for B5'. Resolution: EventBus stays app-side — domain-coupled over the closed AppEvent enum, schema-irrelevant, unrelated to the SSE sync core this chunk scopes. Only the genuinely-generic SSE registry/audience/extractor moved. Framework EventHandler trait (B2) unchanged.

Notify-and-refetch invariant intact (wire carries only entity/action/id — the payload types didn't move; event.rs wire_payload_is_notify_only_snake_case still guards the 3-field shape). Owner isolation + perm gating semantics byte-preserved; admin short-circuit still separate from has_permission (not folded).
